name: Hooke's law — linear elastic stress-strain relation
principles: linear elasticity — stress is proportional to strain in the small-deformation limit
first-order Taylor expansion of an elastic potential energy around the unstressed reference state
isotropic continuum mechanics; generalised tensorial form for anisotropic media
conventions: uniaxial form: sigma is the engineering stress (N/m^2 = Pa) and epsilon = Delta L / L_0 is the engineering strain (dimensionless)
E is Young's modulus (Pa) — the slope of the linear region of the stress-strain curve
general form: sigma_ij is the Cauchy stress tensor; epsilon_kl is the small-strain (infinitesimal) tensor (epsilon_kl = (1/2)(partial_k u_l + partial_l u_k)); C_ijkl is the rank-4 stiffness tensor

isotropic specialisation: C_ijkl = lambda delta_ij delta_kl + mu (delta_ik delta_jl + delta_il delta_jk) with Lamé parameters lambda, mu; equivalently parameterised by Young's modulus E and Poisson's ratio nu
linear range only: valid up to the proportional / yield limit; beyond that, plastic deformation, hysteresis, and ultimate failure dominate
Einstein summation convention applied to repeated indices in the tensorial form
expectedLimits: uniform uniaxial loading of an isotropic bar: epsilon_axial = sigma / E; lateral contraction epsilon_lateral = -nu sigma / E
shear-only loading of an isotropic medium: tau = G gamma with shear modulus G = E / (2(1 + nu))
hydrostatic pressure: epsilon_volumetric = -p / K with bulk modulus K = E / (3(1 - 2 nu)); incompressible limit nu -> 1/2 sends K -> infinity
elastic energy density: w = (1/2) sigma_ij epsilon_ij — quadratic-positive-definite in strain, recovers w = (1/2) E epsilon^2 for uniaxial isotropic
small-strain limit |epsilon| << 1 (typically <~ 0.1-0.5% for metals before yield): linear regime valid; beyond yield the J2 plasticity / Ramberg-Osgood corrections take over
anisotropic crystals (cubic / hexagonal): C_ijkl reduces to 3 / 5 independent constants by symmetry; isotropic equivalents are weighted averages (Voigt / Reuss / Hill)
frequency limit: quasi-static; at high frequencies viscoelastic / inertial corrections (sigma = E epsilon + eta dot epsilon) are required
references: Hooke (1678) — De Potentia Restitutiva ("Ut tensio sic vis")
Young (1807) — A Course of Lectures on Natural Philosophy and the Mechanical Arts
Landau & Lifshitz — Theory of Elasticity (Course of Theoretical Physics, vol. 7), ch.1
Timoshenko & Goodier — Theory of Elasticity, ch.1-2
ASTM E111 — Standard Test Method for Young's Modulus, Tangent Modulus, and Chord Modulus
